question: What are the most common vulnerability classes auditors find in Soroban smart contracts?

golden.answer: Treat this as a non-exhaustive Soroban review checklist: missing/wrong authorization (including initialization and Wasm updates); unsafe storage/TTL/archival assumptions; unchecked external token/contract results or arbitrary counterparties; arithmetic/precision/input-validation errors; panic/unwrap and unbounded-work denial of service; and application-specific economic/state-transition mistakes. Under P23+ archived Persistent/Instance data can auto-restore with correct transaction metadata, so do not present generic 'restore front-running' or irreversible-brick slogans as universal current behavior. Over-limit writes fail atomically, though growth-dependent application paths can still become unavailable.
keyFacts: Names the major current authorization, storage, external-call, arithmetic/input, DoS, and economic/state-transition families.
Scopes storage/TTL issues to current P23+ restoration and atomic failure semantics.
avoid: Do NOT claim one checklist is exhaustive or import EVM-only vulnerabilities unchanged.
Do NOT repeat pre-P23 manual-only restoration or automatic permanent-brick claims.
golden.notes:
2026-06-29: removed duplicated Why-these-cards/Edge sections (verified 2026-06-29). Routes to scout_research (audit corpus). Trap: pasting the EVM vuln list. Verified against Veridise Soroban checklist + Inferara Soroban Security Portal; reentrancy into the same contract is disallowed by the host.
Also good if the answer: Notes that classic Ethereum-style reentrancy is largely mitigated by Soroban's host model (no reentry into the same contract).
Golden cites: A Soroban security checklist/portal (Veridise blog, Inferara portal) or SDF security docs.
GT-48 CORRECTION 2026-07-11: keep vulnerability taxonomy non-exhaustive and current to P23+ restoration.

corroboration.claim: Current recurring review targets include auth/upgrade/init errors, storage/TTL assumptions, unchecked external results/counterparties, arithmetic/input/panic/unbounded-work DoS, and application economic/state-transition bugs; P23 auto-restore changes older restore-front-running framing.

verified.by: Golden-truth sweep import — The inherited vulnerability list repeated obsolete/generalized TTL-front-running and permanent-brick language. Current P23+ auto-restore and atomic limit failure require narrower storage/availability guidance.
evidence: Solo scratchpad 575 GT-46/48 primary and blind reviews; GT-48 independently confirmed the list but required TTL wording correction.
rootCause: Plain protocol drift from pre-P23 restoration semantics and unsupported irreversible-brick shorthand.
